description: Hand-built 3-block circuit: MLP0 looks up place value per digit pos; Attn1 sums to N; MLP1 computes all 8 bits via 255 clipped-step ReLU pairs; MLP2 gates bit-b to the prediction at position p=10-b; head reads bit logit. LayerNorms replaced with Identity.
Same circuit as v1 but d_model shrunk to 37 (exact channel budget): 11 token + 12 pos + 1 digit-pos flag + 1 place-value + 1 N + 8 bits + 2 logit accumulators + 1 attention lane. ~33k params.

2-block circuit. Block 0 attention's softmax IS the place-value weighting (k_j = log(place(j)) on digit positions, -inf elsewhere); attended * 111 = N. Block 0 MLP: 255 clipped-step ReLU pairs over N produce all 8 bits. Block 1 MLP: 16 AND-gates select bit_(10-p) at prediction position p. LayerNorms replaced with Identity. No place-value channel, no MLP0 lookup.

Divide-and-conquer: 8 stacked blocks, each extracting one bit by clipped_step(curN, 2^k) and subtracting 2^k * bit from curN. Block 0 also pools the digits to N via log-place attention (as in v3). A 9th block runs the position-gating ANDs into the head. Each bit-extracting MLP has just 2 hidden neurons.

8-block divide-and-conquer with fused position gating: each block has 4 hidden neurons (2 for the unconditional clipped-step extraction + curN decrement, 2 for an AND-with-position that writes the same bit to logit_1 if and only if we are at the matching generation position). No separate gating block. Head adds a position-conditional '0' baseline.
Same circuit as v5 (8-block divide-and-conquer with fused position gating) but with the 7 unused attention pathways replaced by a zero-parameter _ZeroAttn module. Drops ~36k dead parameters.

Same circuit as v6 (fused-DnQ with zero-attn modules) but with d_model shrunk to 34: dropped the unused is_digit_pos channel and overlapped the attention scoring/value lane with the logit-for-'1' channel (attention reads/writes neither, so the overlap is safe).
Same circuit as v7 but max_seq_len shrunk from 16 to exactly 12 (prompt_len 4 + answer_len 8). No other change.

Same circuit as v8 but Block 0's attention is a custom 1-lane _ScalarLaneAttn (4 vectors of size d_model, 136 params total) instead of the full 4·d_model^2 (4624 params) projection. Mathematically equivalent to v8's attention with the LANE row of each W_*, but eliminates ~4.5k dead parameters.
Same D&Q circuit as v9 but drops the 8 unused per-bit channels (CH_BIT0..7) -- bits were written but never read. Shrinks d_model from 34 to 26 and removes the bit-write rows from every MLP's fc2.

Replaces each D&Q bit-block's vanilla MLP(d_model, 4) with a custom _SparseBitMLP that reads only 2 residual channels (curN + the matching pos one-hot) and writes only 3 (curN + 2 logits), storing 27 params instead of 238. Behaves identically to v10.

Same D&Q circuit as v11 but replaces token/pos embeddings with a parameter-free one-hot lookup (_OneHotEmbedding) and replaces the Linear head with a parameter-free _SparseHead that hard-codes the '0'/'1' readout and position baseline. Drops 884 dead params.

Same circuit as v12 but Block 0's attention is replaced with a parameter-free _DigitPlaceAttn that hand-rolls the place-value pooling (causal cumulative sum of digit*place). Equivalent to a log-place softmax attention with frozen weights; drops 104 params.

Same circuit as v13 (192 params, 8-block D&Q with sparse MLPs and parameter-free attention/embeddings/head). Promoted as a stress test at n_samples=2000 to confirm exact robustness across many random N values.

Same circuit as v14 but _SparseBitMLP stores fc1/fc2 as **buffers** instead of nn.Parameter, since every coefficient is a hand-derived constant. The whole transformer now has ZERO learned parameters; every computation is hard-wired.
Same zero-param circuit as v15 but L_GATE is tightened from 1000 to 256 (just above max curN=255). Tests numerical tightness of the position-AND gating trick.

Same zero-param circuit as v16 but LOGIT_SCALE drops from 10 to 1.0 -- the absolute minimum that still produces an unambiguous argmax (logit gap is exactly 1.0). Demonstrates the head needs no temperature, just the right sign.

Same zero-param circuit as v17 but moves the head's '1' baseline into the per-block MLPs: each MLP adds a 5th hidden ReLU that passes pos_onehot[p_out] through to LOGIT0, so the per-block output at p_out is (1 - bit) on LOGIT0 and bit on LOGIT1. The head becomes a pure linear readout (no position-aware baseline).
Same zero-param circuit as v18; rerun under eval seed=42 (instead of the default seed=0) at n_samples=500 to confirm correctness is dataset-independent (since the model is fully closed-form).

FINAL: 8-block divide-and-conquer transformer with ZERO learned parameters. Block 0 uses parameter-free _DigitPlaceAttn to pool N=100*d0+10*d1+d2 into CH_N. Blocks 0..7 each apply a hard-coded _SparseBitMLP (5 ReLUs) that extracts one bit via clipped_step, decrements curN, and (at the matching output position) writes (1-bit) and bit to LOGIT0/LOGIT1. Pos-aware AND uses L_GATE=256. Token/pos embeddings are one-hot lookups; head is a sparse linear readout. Stress test at n_samples=5000.
